content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

It is guaranteed that AAA contains **at least two distinct** elements.

You are also given a non-negative integer XXX.

For each YYY (0≤Y≤X)(0 \leq Y \leq X)(0≤Y≤X), we create an array BBB of size NNN such that Bi=Ai∣YB_i = A_i | YBi​=Ai​∣Y, where ∣|∣ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

Find the **largest** integer Y(Y≤X)Y(Y \leq X)Y(Y≤X) such that the array BBB contains **at least two distinct** elements.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX — the length of the array AAA and maximum value of YYY allowed.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​,  representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the largest YYY satisfying the condition.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X. The second line contains N space‑separated non‑negative integers A1, A2, …, AN. It is guaranteed that the array contains at least two distinct elements and 0 ≤ Ai, X < 2^30.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the largest integer Y (0 ≤ Y ≤ X) such that after replacing every Ai by Ai | Y the resulting array contains at least two distinct values.

solution.approach: The answer can be built greedily from the most significant bit to the least significant bit. We maintain a current candidate ans. For each bit b from 29 down to 0 we try to set this bit (cand = ans | (1<<b)). If cand ≤ X and using this cand the array after OR still has at least two distinct values, we keep the bit (ans = cand). The check is O(N): compute first = A1|cand and verify that some other Ai|cand differs. Because we try at most 30 bits, the total per test case is O(N·30) which fits the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·log MAX_BIT) per test case (log MAX_BIT = 30), overall O((sum N)·30).
